skill.name: hyperframes-media
skill.description: Asset preprocessing for HyperFrames compositions — text-to-speech narration (Kokoro), audio/video transcription (Whisper), and background removal for transparent overlays (u2net). Use when generating voiceover from text, transcribing speech for captions, removing the background from a video or image to use as a transparent overlay, choosing a TTS voice or whisper model, or chaining these (TTS → transcribe → captions). Each command downloads its own model on first run.
skill.summary: Generate TTS narration, transcribe audio with word-level timestamps, and remove video/image backgrounds for transparent overlays — all locally, no API keys needed.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: All 144 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanned file (SKILL.md) is a markdown documentation file, not executable code. Backtick patterns flagged as 'Ruby/shell execution' are shell commands inside markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples. Filesystem and hidden file path alerts reference legitimate model cache directories (~/.cache/hyperframes/). Weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance alerts are text matching errors against model names, language codes, and documentation references. The skill poses no security risk as it only instructs users to run CLI commands with no data exfiltration, network callbacks, or hidden behavior.

medium_findings.title: External commands in documentation code blocks
medium_findings.description: False positive. SKILL.md is a markdown documentation file. Backtick patterns flagged by the static analyzer are shell commands inside markdown fenced code blocks. These are CLI usage examples showing users how to run npx hyperframes commands for legitimate media preprocessing tasks. No executable code is present. This is standard practice for CLI tool documentation.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem access to home directory cache
medium_findings.description: False positive. References to ~/.cache/hyperframes/ are legitimate model cache directory paths used by ML pipelines. Models (Kokoro TTS, Whisper, u2net) are downloaded on first run and cached locally. This is standard behavior for local ML tools and poses no security risk as only model weights are stored.

low_findings.title: Path traversal sequence in markdown link
low_findings.description: False positive. The '..' path at line 90 is a relative markdown link reference to '../hyperframes/references/transcript-guide.md'. This is a documentation cross-reference, not a path traversal attack. The pattern is a standard relative URL in markdown files.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm match
low_findings.description: False positive. The static analyzer misidentified model names (u2net, small.en, medium), version numbers, and language codes as cryptographic algorithm references. These are model identifiers and configuration values, not cryptographic primitives. No actual weak crypto usage exists in this documentation file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance match
low_findings.description: False positive. References at line 41 and 128 are documentation about system requirements (espeak-ng dependency) and output format compatibility (webm/mov/png). These are informational notes, not reconnaissance commands. No system enumeration is performed.

content.user_title: Preprocess media assets for video compositions
content.value_statement: Creating video compositions requires voiceovers, captions, and transparent overlays, but setting up ML pipelines for each task is complex. This skill generates TTS narration, transcribes audio with word-level timestamps, and removes video backgrounds through simple CLI commands that work on your local machine.
actual_capabilities: Generate TTS narration audio using Kokoro-82M with 54 voices across 9 languages
Transcribe audio and video files to JSON with word-level timestamps using Whisper
Remove backgrounds from video files and images using u2net human segmentation
Extract foreground and background layers separately for compositing workflows
Import existing SRT, VTT, and OpenAI transcription formats for normalization
Chain TTS output into transcription for automatically timed captions
limitations: Requires Python 3.8+ and pip packages (kokoro-onnx, soundfile) for TTS functionality
Model downloads are large (311 MB for TTS, up to 3.1 GB for whisper large-v3) on first run
Background removal produces hole-cut plates, not inpainted clean backgrounds
Non-English TTS phonemization requires espeak-ng system package

use_cases.title: Add voiceover narration to a product demo video
use_cases.description: A developer building a product demo composition needs a voiceover track. They write the script, generate TTS audio with a professional voice, then reference the audio file in their composition HTML.

use_cases.title: Auto-generate captions for accessibility
use_cases.description: A content creator needs word-level captions for a tutorial video. They transcribe the audio with Whisper, get precise timestamps, and use the output JSON to render animated captions overlaid on the video.

use_cases.title: Isolate a presenter for transparent overlay compositing
use_cases.description: A video editor has a talking-head recording and wants to overlay the presenter over custom graphics. They run background removal to produce a transparent WebM, then layer graphics between the background plate and the cutout.

prompt_templates.title: Generate TTS narration from a script file
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a script saved in script.txt. Generate TTS narration using Kokoro with a warm professional voice, output to narration.wav.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a written script and needs voiceover audio for their composition.

prompt_templates.title: Transcribe audio with word-level timestamps
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe the file interview.mp3 using Whisper. The audio is in Spanish, so use the small model with language es.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has recorded audio and needs a transcript with precise word timing for captions.

prompt_templates.title: Remove background from presenter video for overlay
prompt_templates.prompt: Remove the background from subject.mp4 to create a transparent WebM cutout at balanced quality. Also extract the background plate as plate.webm so I can place text between them.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a talking-head recording and wants to use it as a transparent overlay in a composition.

prompt_templates.title: Full pipeline: TTS to captions workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a complete voiceover with captions. First generate TTS from script.txt using a clear British English voice, then transcribe the output to get word-level timestamps for animated captions.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to generate both narration audio and matching captions in a single workflow.

output_examples.input: A developer runs TTS generation on a product demo script with a professional voice.
output_examples.output: A WAV audio file containing synthesized speech narration. The voice sounds warm and professional, matching the recommended voice for product demo content.

output_examples.input: A video editor runs background removal on a talking-head MP4 recording to create a transparent overlay.
output: A transparent VP9 WebM file where the person appears with a fully transparent background, ready to layer over other content in a composition.
A separate background plate WebM with the inverse alpha channel, showing the original surroundings with a transparent hole where the subject was.

output_examples.input: A content creator transcribes a tutorial video to get word-level captions for accessibility.
output_examples.output: A JSON transcript file with word-level timestamps listing each spoken word, its start time in seconds, its end time in seconds, and a stable ID for referencing in caption animations.

best_practices: Match TTS voice to content type: use warm voices like af_nova for demos, neutral voices like am_adam for tutorials, and energetic voices like af_sky for marketing content.
Always specify the --language flag for non-English transcription to prevent Whisper from silently translating the audio into English.
Wrap cutout overlay videos in a non-timed parent div and animate the wrapper opacity instead of the video element to avoid the framework overriding opacity on active clips.
anti_patterns: Do not use .en Whisper models for non-English audio, as they silently translate instead of transcribe, destroying the original language content.
Do not use --background-output when the user needs an inpainted clean plate (a room shown without the person) - this command only produces a hole-cut plate with a transparent subject-shaped gap.
Do not place the cutout overlay over a different take of the same person, as the re-encoded RGB will look like two separate overlapping people.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use TTS or transcription?
faq.answer: No. Both Kokoro TTS and Whisper transcription run entirely on your local machine with downloaded models. No API keys or cloud services are required.

faq.question: How large are the model downloads on first run?
faq.answer: Kokoro TTS downloads about 338 MB total. Whisper models range from 75 MB (tiny) to 3.1 GB (large-v3). The background removal model is about 168 MB. Models are cached in ~/.cache/hyperframes/ and reused.

faq.question: What languages does TTS support?
faq.answer: Kokoro supports 9 languages: American English, British English, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Japanese, Brazilian Portuguese, and Mandarin. The voice ID prefix encodes the language.

faq.question: Can I remove the background from a still image?
faq.answer: Yes, you can process single images. Use npx hyperframes remove-background portrait.jpg -o cutout.png to produce a PNG cutout with a transparent background.

faq.question: What transcription formats can be imported?
faq.answer: You can import SRT, VTT, and OpenAI JSON response formats. These are normalized into the standard word-level timestamp JSON format used by HyperFrames compositions.

faq.question: What system dependencies are needed for non-English TTS?
faq.answer: Non-English phonemization requires espeak-ng to be installed system-wide. Install with brew install espeak-ng on macOS or apt-get install espeak-ng on Linux.
